commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=30ae5af9fab11d3523d39b11de4baf23d0187904)
commands.review_summary:
The PR rewrites the shared non-DM auto-reply prompt opener to avoid declaring a `group chat` type and updates prompt tests and a Codex prompt snapshot.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +16. Total +22 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: a Mattermost public channel maps to `chat_type: channel`, but current main still routes that context through an opener that says `group chat`; no live Mattermost run was performed in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to provide inspectable real-behavior proof and fix the generated snapshot drift before this otherwise narrow bug fix can merge.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): use a chat_type-neutral group/channel intro opener
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #95645.

`buildGroupChatContext` is built for **both** `group` and `channel` ChatTypes (the caller gates on `isGroupChat = ChatType === "group" || ChatType === "channel"` in `get-reply-run`), but its opener hard-coded:

```
You are in a <provider> group chat.
```

For a **public channel** the authoritative inbound-meta block reports `chat_type: "channel"`, while `"group"` denotes a private channel / group DM. So the natural-language opener contradicts the trusted metadata, and "group chat" is the more salient signal. Agents key off it and emit `chat_type: group` (or `--chat-type group` to tools) for a public channel.

Because session keys are namespaced `…:mattermost:<direct|channel|group>:<peerId>:thread:<root>`, a wrong `group` for a public channel addresses a **non-existent** session and breaks downstream routing.

## Fix

Reword the opener so it no longer declares a `chat_type`:

```diff
-  lines.push(`You are in a ${providerLabel} group chat.`);
+  lines.push(`You are in a shared ${providerLabel} chat (not a 1:1 DM).`);
```

This is the neutral wording proposed in the issue. It is **provider-agnostic** — `buildGroupChatContext` is shared by all providers, and "shared chat (not a 1:1 DM)" holds for every non-DM conversation this intro fires for. The remaining `"group"` mentions in the function ("be a good group participant", "…sent to this group chat") are colloquial role/delivery guidance, not a type declaration, so they are intentionally left unchanged.

## Scope

Deliberately narrow: this is the system-prompt-intro half only. The deeper `chat_type`-derivation inconsistency for private channels (channel-type vs target-string inference) is tracked separately in #95646 and is **not** addressed here.

## What changed

- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts` — neutral opener + rationale comment.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` — updated expectation; **new regression test** asserting a public-channel (`ChatType: "channel"`) context opens neutrally and never says "… group chat".
- `src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.group-intro-prompts.cases.ts` — updated the Discord/WhatsApp/Telegram opener expectations (delivery-guidance text unchanged).
- `src/agents/prompt-composition.test.ts` — updated the Slack group opener invariant.
- `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/discord-group-codex-message-tool.md` — regenerated opener line in the committed Codex prompt snapshot.

## Evidence

- **Source-level repro:** the failing path is exactly as the issue describes — a Mattermost `O` (public) channel maps to `chat_type: "channel"`, which `get-reply-run` routes through `buildGroupChatContext`, which emitted the "group chat" opener that collides with the trusted metadata.
- **Focused test (added):** the new `groups.test.ts` case fails before the change (the channel opener contains "Mattermost group chat") and passes after (`You are in a shared Mattermost chat (not a 1:1 DM).`, and `not.toContain("You are in a Mattermost group chat.")`). The case/snapshot/invariant updates keep the rest of the suite green.
- **Live production observation:** this exact reword has been running in our deployment (`ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw` + a one-line `sed` over `get-reply-*.js`). Before the patch, threaded cron delivery on a public Mattermost channel fail-closed because the agent emitted `chat_type=group` and bound a non-existent `group:<channelId>` session; after the patch the agent reports `chat_type: channel` and threaded delivery routes correctly.
target.author: iloveleon19
